title: Branch, or Layer? Zeroth-Order Optimization for Continual Learning of Vision-Language Models
abstract: The paper explores zeroth-order optimization for continual learning in vision-language models, investigating whether ZO optimization should be applied at the branch level or layer level. The authors find that naive full-ZO optimization causes divergence, but collaborative FO-ZO approaches at both branch and layer levels show promise, with certain configurations surpassing full-FO baselines.
key_findings: Naive full-ZO optimization causes training divergence and failure to converge
Collaborative FO-ZO approaches at branch level significantly outperform naive full-ZO
Collaborative FO-ZO approaches at layer level yield better results, with some configurations surpassing full-FO baseline
Visual branch exhibits stronger fluctuations under ZO than language branch
A stabilization mechanism for the visual branch improves optimization convergence and continual learning performance
research_questions: Can ZO and FO collaborate for global model optimization across branches?
Can ZO and FO collaborate for hierarchical optimization within branch layers?
